english: to raise; to lift; to exalt

etymology.description: semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 昜(昜) -> to raise or lift using hands
etymology.interpretation: The character 揚(揚) involves a hand 手(手) to convey the act of raising or lifting. The phonetic component 昜(昜) gives the sound, aligning with the action of raising or lifting a hand.
etymology.simplification: Simplified by replacing the phonetic component 昜(昜) with a simpler component 𠃓, reducing strokes while maintaining the overall meaning.

examples.chinese: 风把尘土扬起来(風把塵土揚起來)。
他在演讲中扬起了全国的热情(他在演講中揚起了全國的熱情)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài yǎnjiǎng zhōng yáng qǐle quánguó de rèqíng.
examples.english: He stirred up national enthusiasm in his speech.

examples.chinese: 旗帜在风中迎风飘扬(旗幟在風中迎風飄揚)。
examples.pinyin: Qízhì zài fēng zhōng yíngfēng piāoyáng.
examples.english: The flag flutters in the wind.
